description: The wave: the sea's shape when it moves. Third member of the 'mori-' family, after 'moriwhe' (sea) and 'morisa' (salt). A wave is not something the sea has; it is something the sea briefly does.
sound_symbolism: The sea-syllables 'mo'-'ri' end in liquid 'lu': the family signature set moving.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'morilu mua kirua lepa.' (The wave falls on the sand.) 'moriwhe morilu phelu.' (The sea holds waves.)

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The wave is the teaching about self that requires no translation: briefly distinct, never separate, and the sea loses nothing when it goes.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: The curling crest is one of the style's inherited obsessions, an organic curve that draws itself and never twice the same.
